document_title: DIPLOMA IN TEACHER EDUCATION: CHILD DEVELOPMENT AND PSYCHOLOGY CURRICULUM DESIGN (PRE-PRIMARY AND PRIMARY)

metadata.title: DIPLOMA IN TEACHER EDUCATION: PRE-PRIMARY AND PRIMARY, CHILD DEVELOPMENT AND PSYCHOLOGY CURRICULUM DESIGN

table_of_content.section: REGULATIONS FOR DIPLOMA IN TEACHER EDUCATION - PRE-PRIMARY AND PRIMARY (DTE–PP&P)
TABLE 1: DISTRIBUTION OF PROFESSIONAL LEARNING AREAS
STRAND 1.0 INTRODUCTION TO CHILD GROWTH AND DEVELOPMENT
STRAND 2.0 THEORIES OF GROWTH AND DEVELOPMENT

introduction.curriculum_goal: The development of the curriculum for Diploma in Teacher Education for the Pre-Primary and Primary level (DTE–PP&P) is a critical milestone in the implementation of Competency Based Curriculum (CBC) in Kenya [4, 5].
introduction.target_learners: The curriculum designs have been developed to prepare the student teacher to be able to effectively guide the learners at the Pre-Primary School and Primary School level; that is from Pre-Primary One (PP1) to Grade Six (G6) in Basic Education [5].
introduction.curriculum_shift: The DTE–PP&P embraces the shift from the Objective-Based to the Competency Based Curriculum which is hinged on use of learner-centred pedagogy for realisation of the stated expected learning outcomes [5].
key_aspects_introduced: Emphasis on formative assessment to facilitate learning and determine learner aptitude and performance [6].
A longer period for the practicum [6].

Introduction of specific Professional Courses that ensure congruence with the CBC vision, mission, pillars and guiding principles as enshrined in the Basic Education Curriculum Framework (KICD, 2017) [6].
Emphasis on experiential and reflective learning to develop appropriate Pedagogical Content Knowledge (PCK) [7].
Integrated content and pedagogy for the student teachers while at college [7].
introduction.teacher_role: The DTE-PP&P curriculum seeks to develop the teacher to act as a facilitator in the learning process taking into consideration the different abilities and learning styles of individual target learners [7].

professional_learning_areas: Sociological and Philosophical Foundations of Education [8]
Historical and Comparative Foundations of Education [9]
integrated_content_and_pedagogy_learning: Foreign Languages: French/ Arabic/ German/ Mandarin (Chinese)/ KSL [9]

regulations.title: REGULATIONS FOR DIPLOMA IN TEACHER EDUCATION - PRE-PRIMARY AND PRIMARY (DTE–PP&P)

entry_requirements.standard: C Plain Mean Grade in the Kenya Certificate of Secondary Education examination (KCSE) or its equivalent (as equated by the Kenya National Examinations Council (KNEC) [10].
entry_requirements.special_needs_candidates_sne: C Minus (-) Grade in KCSE or equivalent [10].

regulations.duration_of_training: The duration for the DTE–PP&P shall be three years [10].
regulations.subjects_offered: The trainee undertaking the DTE–PP&P shall take ALL courses specified in the DPTE curriculum; which includes Professional Courses and learning areas (subjects) in Primary School Curriculum [11].

micro_teaching_and_practicum.micro_teaching_status: Micro Teaching shall be undertaken as a course and shall be a pre-requisite for the Practicum; hence a course design has been developed for it [11].
micro_teaching_and_practicum.practicum_duration: There shall be two (2) school term practicum sessions for which guidelines shall be developed [11].

requirements: Complete the required hours for coursework and pass the stipulated assessment as directed by the Kenya National Examinations Council (KNEC) [11, 12].
Complete the required hours for the Practicum and pass the stipulated assessment as directed by the Kenya National Examinations Council (KNEC) [12].
award_of_diploma.failure_policy: If the student fails to meet the requirements for award of the Diploma in Teacher Education –Pre-Primary and Primary, he/she will be allowed to repeat the specific component or learning area failed [12].

regulations.grading: The DTE–PP&P shall be graded as stipulated by the Kenya National Examinations Council (KNEC) [12].

courses.subject: Sociological and Philosophical Foundations of Education
Historical and Comparative Foundations of Education

subjects.subject: Foreign Languages: French/ Arabic/ German/ Mandarin (Chinese)/ KSL

national_goals_of_education: Foster nationalism and patriotism and promote national unity [16, 17].
Promote the social, economic, technological and industrial needs for national development [18]. (Includes sub-goals: Social Needs, Economic Needs, Technological and Industrial Needs) [18-20].
Promote individual development and self-fulfilment (including building of character) [20].
Promote sound moral and religious values [20, 21].
Promote respect for and development of Kenya’s rich and varied cultures [22].
Promote international consciousness and foster positive attitudes towards other nations [22, 23].
Promote positive attitudes towards good health and environmental protection [23, 24].
level_learning_outcomes: Model appropriate behaviour and values for primary school learners to emulate for development of good citizenship [24].
Communicate and collaborate effectively with learners, peers, parents and the community to create a conducive learning environment [24].
Use appropriate pedagogical approaches to facilitate learning for primary school learners in and out of the classroom [24, 25].
Apply inclusive practices to support all primary school learners including those with disabilities and special educational needs [25].
Employ ICT skills in the learning process to enhance digital literacy [25].
Employ appropriate assessment approaches to promote effective learning [25].
Identify and nurture learner’s potential and talents for appropriate placement and transition into lower secondary education [25].
Develop environmental conservation skills in primary school learners to promote education for sustainable development [25, 26].
Create innovative and effective solutions to challenges in the learning process [26].
Integrate pertinent and contemporary issues in learning to enable learners to cope with daily challenges [26].
educational_goals_and_outcomes.essence_statement: This course on Child Development and Psychology deals with how children grow physically, mentally, emotionally, and socially [26]. Awareness of these facets is crucial for a primary school teacher, equipping the trainee with knowledge about children’s ability at different ages [26, 27]. The course is designed to equip and develop knowledge and skills that would enable the teacher trainee effectively guide the young learners [27]. It provides knowledge on different aspects of growth and development of the child and the requirements at different stages of development [27]. It addresses the fact that every child has different mental ability and learns at different pace levels [28]. Child psychology helps the teacher to study the ability, interests, intelligence, and needs of the child as well as the different techniques of teaching for effective communication [28].
general_learning_outcomes: Analyse major developmental milestones of children in the areas of physical, cognitive, and emotional development [29].
Compare and contrast various theoretical frameworks that relate to the study of human development [29].
Evaluate the effectiveness of child development theories in terms of their practical applications in learning [29].
Analyse the effect of biological, environmental and cultural influences on development of children [29].
Apply appropriate theoretical approaches when analysing aspects of child growth and development [29].

curriculum_strands_detailed.title: INTRODUCTION TO CHILD GROWTH AND DEVELOPMENT

sub_strands.concept: Concepts in Child Growth and Development
specific_learning_outcomes: examine terminologies related to child growth and development [30]
analyse the brain development in children from 0- 5 years [30]
illustrate the responsibilities of caregivers in promoting normal brain development [31]
recognise the concepts of growth and development of children in the learning process [31]
suggested_learning_experiences: search and discuss the information on terminologies (growth, development, personality, memory, intelligence, attitude, motivation, emotions, learning, physical, cognitive, social, and emotional development in children) related to child growth and development. Share findings in class [31]
study and brainstorm brain development in children from 0-5 years. (factors that influence, and process) [31, 32]
simulate the role of caregivers in enhancing normal brain development [32]
watch a demonstration or an audio visual clip on care of young children in different contexts such as crèches [32]
suggested_key_inquiry_questions: Why is it important for a teacher to study child growth and development? [32]
How do caregivers enhance normal brain development? [32]
sub_strands.core_competencies_developed: Digital literacy skills: Teacher trainee demonstrates digital literacy skills as they search for information on terminologies related to child growth and development [32, 33].
sub_strands.values: Responsibility: Teacher trainee engages in assigned roles and duties while simulating on the roles of caregivers in enhancing normal brain development [33].

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to examine terminologies related to child growth and development
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Clearly examines terminologies related to child growth and development [33]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Examines terminologies related to child growth and development [34]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Partially examines terminologies related to child growth and development [34]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Requires assistance to examine terminologies related to child growth and development [34]

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to analyse the brain development in children from 0- 5 years
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Comprehensively analyses the brain development in children from 0- 5 years [34, 35]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Analyses the brain development in children from 0- 5 years [35]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Analyses the brain development in children from 0- 5 years that require clarity [35]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Analysis made on the brain development in children from 0- 5 years require clarity [35]

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to illustrate the responsibilities of caregivers in promoting normal brain development
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Accurately illustrates the responsibilities of caregivers in promoting normal brain development [35, 36]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Illustrates the responsibilities of caregivers in promoting normal brain development [36]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Illustrates few responsibilities of caregivers in promoting normal brain development [36]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Inaccurately illustrates the responsibilities of caregivers in promoting normal brain development [36]

specific_learning_outcomes: examine changes in children from infancy to adolescence [36]
evaluate factors influencing child growth and development [37]
illustrate the characteristics of children from infancy to adolescence [37]
acknowledge the effect of developmental changes on children's learning process [37]
suggested_learning_experiences: search and discuss the information on changes (biological, cognitive, physical, socio-emotional) in children from infancy to adolescence (infants, toddlers, pre-schoolers, school age (age 6-10) and adolescents) Share findings in class [37]

study and brainstorm the factors that influence child growth and development (biological factors - genetic inheritances, brain chemistry, hormone levels, nutrition, and gender; environmental factors; socio-cultural factors; role of play; care of children) [37, 38]
demonstrate the traits of children (ages 0-12) that influence the learning process [38]
suggested_key_inquiry_questions: How do developmental changes vary across stages from childhood to adolescence? [38]
sub_strands.core_competencies_developed: Pedagogical Content Knowledge: Teacher trainee demonstrates appreciation of the experience, as they watch an audiovisual clip or a demonstration on care of young children in different contexts [38, 39].
sub_strands.values: Unity: Teacher trainee collaborate with others as they demonstrate the traits of children (ages 0-12) that influence the learning process [39].

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to examine changes in children from infancy to adolescence
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Comprehensively examine changes in children from infancy to adolescence [39]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Analyses changes in children from infancy to adolescence [40]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Partially analyses changes in children from infancy to adolescence [40]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Leaves out many details in analyses of changes in children from infancy to adolescence [40]

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to evaluate factors influencing child growth and development
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Comprehensively evaluate factors influencing child growth and development [40]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Evaluates factors influencing child growth and development [40]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Evaluates some factors influencing child growth and development [40]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Omits many details when evaluating factors influencing child growth and development [41]

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to illustrate the characteristics of children from infancy to adolescence
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Distinctively illustrates the characteristics of children from infancy to adolescence [41]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Illustrates the characteristics of children from infancy to adolescence [41]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Illustrates some characteristics of children from infancy to adolescence [41]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Illustrates unclear characteristics of children from infancy to adolescence [41]

specific_learning_outcomes: examine personality development in relation to child growth and development [42]
apply psychoanalytic theory in the development process of children [42]
propose the personality development challenges experienced by learners [42]
appreciate the role of theories of personality in children's learning process [43]
suggested_learning_experiences: study and discuss the information on structures, theories, types, and personality traits according to psychoanalytic theory (Sigmund Freud). Share your findings in class [43]
create a scenario to act out different situations aligned to psychoanalytic theory. Reflect and share results in class [43]
brainstorm and identify the personality development challenges experienced by learners [43]
suggested_key_inquiry_questions: Why should a teacher study personality development theories? [44]
sub_strands.core_competencies_developed: Creativity and innovation: Teacher trainee creates a conducive learning environment and displays a scenario to act out different situations aligned to psychoanalytic theory [44].
sub_strands.values: Love: Teacher trainee displays respect as they brainstorm the personality development challenges experienced by learners [45].

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to examine personality development in relation to child growth and development
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Clearly examines personality development in relation to child growth and development [45]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Examines personality development in relation to child growth and development [45]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Partially examines personality development in relation to child growth and development [45]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Examines personality development in relation to child growth and development with details that require clarity [45]

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to apply psychoanalytic theory in the development process of children
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Comprehensively applies psychoanalytic theory in the development process of children [46]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Applies apply psychoanalytic theory in the development process of children [46]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Omits some details when applying psychoanalytic theory in the development process of children [46]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Omits many details when applying psychoanalytic theory in the development process of children [46]

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to propose the personality development challenges experienced by learners
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Accurately proposes the personality development challenges experienced by learners [46, 47]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Proposes the personality development challenges experienced by learners [47]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Proposes few personality development challenges experienced by learners that are inaccurate [47]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Many proposed personality development challenges experienced by learners require clarity [47]

specific_learning_outcomes: analyse theories of cognitive development in relation to child growth and development [48]
apply the principles of cognitive development theories to enhance learning in children [48]
appreciate the role of cognitive development theories in the child development process [48]
suggested_learning_experiences: investigate the information on structures, theories, types, and traits in Cognitive development theory (Jean Piaget’s and Vygotsky stages of development). share their findings in class [48, 49]
design activities that can be used to enhance cognitive development in young children and present in class [49]
share a case study, experiences, or documentaries on the principles of cognitive development theories [49]
suggested_key_inquiry_questions: Why should a teacher study cognitive development theories? [49]
sub_strands.core_competencies_developed: Critical thinking and Problem Solving: Teacher trainee facilitates exploring new ways of doing things as they design activities that can be used to enhance cognitive development in young children [49].
sub_strands.values: Integrity: Teacher trainee applies laid down procedures as they investigate the principles of cognitive development theories to enhance learning in children [50].

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to analyse theories of cognitive development in relation to child growth and development
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Comprehensively analyses theories of cognitive development in relation to child growth and development [50, 51]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Analyses theories of cognitive development in relation to child growth and development [51]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Analyses few theories of cognitive development in relation to child growth and development that require clarity [51]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: All theories of cognitive development analysed in relation to child growth and development require clarity [51]

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to apply the principles of cognitive development theories to enhance learning in children
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Comprehensively applies the principles of Cognitive development theories to enhance learning in children [51, 52]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Applies the principles of cognitive development theories to enhance learning in children [52]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Leaves out some details when applying principles of cognitive development theories to enhance learning in children [52]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Leaves out many details when applying principles of cognitive development theories to enhance learning in children [52]

specific_learning_outcomes: analyse language development in children [53]
apply the principles of language development theory to enhance acquisition of language in children [53]
examine factors that influence language development in children [53]
appreciate the role of language development theories in promoting acquisition of language in children [53]
suggested_learning_experiences: study and reflect on the stages of the theory of language development and write a paper (B.F. Skinner) [53, 54]
discuss and present how children develop language in reference to the principles of language development [54]
create and demonstrate a practical classroom strategy such as interactive storytelling, group discussions or debates on principles of language development theory [54]
design and model learning resources that enhance language development in children [54]
suggested_key_inquiry_questions: Why should a teacher study language development theory? [54]
How can learners improve their speaking, listening and writing skills? [55]
sub_strands.core_competencies_developed: Learning to learn and reflective practice: Teacher trainee develops reflective skills in learning as they study and reflect on the stage of theory of language development [55].
sub_strands.values: Unity: Teacher trainee appreciates the effort of one another as they design and model learning resources that enhance language development in children [55].

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to analyse language development in children
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Critically analyses language development in children [56]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Analyses language development in children [56]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Leaves out many details when analysing language development in children [56]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Leaves out many details when analysing language development in children [56]

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to examine factors that influence language development in children
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Accurately examines factors that influence language development in children [56, 57]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Examines factors that influence language development in children [57]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Examines few factors that influence language development in children that are inaccurate [57]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Many factors that influence language development in children are inaccurately examined [57]

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to apply the principles of language development theory to enhance acquisition of language in children
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Extensively applies the principles of language development theory to enhance acquisition of language in children [57, 58]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Applies the principles of language development theory to enhance acquisition of language in children [58]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Applies few principles of language development theory to enhance acquisition of language in children [58]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: All principles of language development theory applied have scanty details [58]

specific_learning_outcomes: analyse theories of psychosocial development in children [59]
apply the principles of psychosocial development theories to promote children's learning process [59]
appreciate the significance of psychosocial development theories in enhancing social behaviour among learners [59]
suggested_learning_experiences: investigate information on the stages of psychosocial theory of development (Erik Erickson) [59]
discuss and present stages of psychosocial theory of development and how to apply it in learning to young children [59, 60]
conduct a game of charades by acting out different simple emotions and scenarios related to the psychosocial stages [60]
suggested_key_inquiry_questions: How can teachers use psychosocial development theory to support children's learning? [60]
sub_strands.core_competencies_developed: Pedagogical content knowledge: Teacher trainee displays understanding of psychosocial theory of development as they investigate information on its stages [60].
sub_strands.values: Responsibility: Teacher trainee offers leadership and guidance to others as they conduct a game of charades by acting out different simple emotions and scenarios related to the psychosocial stages [60, 61].

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to analyse theories of psychosocial development in children
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Exhaustively analyse theories of psychosocial development in children [61]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Analyse theories of psychosocial development in children [61]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Partially analyse theories of psychosocial development in children [61]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Leaves out many details when analysing theories of psychosocial development in children [62]

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to apply the principles of psychosocial development theories to promote children's learning process
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Comprehensively applies the principles of psychosocial development theories to promote children's learning process [62]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Partly applies the principles of psychosocial development theories to promote children's learning process [62]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Omits many details when applying principles of psychosocial development theories to promote children's learning process [62]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Applies unclear principles of psychosocial development theories applied to promote children's learning process [63]

specific_learning_outcomes: analyse moral development theory in relation to child growth and development [63]
apply the principles of moral development theory to enhance learning in children [64]
recognize the importance of moral development theory in children's learning [64]
suggested_learning_experiences: study and write a report on how children develop acceptable moral behaviour (Lawrence Kolberg theory) [64]
design activities that can be used to enhance moral development in young children [64]
organise a classroom activity that aligns with the moral development theory [64]
suggested_key_inquiry_questions: Why should a teacher study moral development theory? [64]
sub_strands.core_competencies_developed: Creativity and Innovation: Teacher trainee makes work interesting for themselves and others around them as they organise a classroom activity that aligns with the moral development theory [65].
sub_strands.values: Peace: Teacher trainee observes respect for self and others as they discuss the principles of moral development theory and presentation [65].

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to analyse moral development theory in relation to child growth and development
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Clearly analyses moral development theory in relation to child growth and development [66]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Analyses moral development theory in relation to child growth and development [66]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Partially analyses moral development theory in relation to child growth and development [66]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Leaves out many details when analysing moral development theory in relation to child growth and development [66]

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to apply the principles of moral development theory to enhance learning in children
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Distinctively applies the principles of moral development theory to enhance learning in children [67]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Applies the principles of moral development theory to enhance learning in children [67]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Applies few theories of cognitive development in relation to child growth and development that require clarity [67]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: All theories of cognitive development analysed in relation to child growth and development require clarity [67]

specific_learning_outcomes: analyse cognitive theories in relation to learning [68]
apply the principles of cognitive theories of learning as used to enhance learning in children [68]
appreciate the role of theories of cognitive learning in the learning process [68]
suggested_learning_experiences: study and discuss how principles of cognitive theories of learning (Jean Piaget’s process of learning) enhance learning in children [68, 69]
create age-appropriate activities and materials that engage children in manipulation, problem-solving and critical thinking. Showcase them in the class [69]
suggested_key_inquiry_questions: How can a teacher use cognitive learning theories to enhance learning? [69]
sub_strands.core_competencies_developed: Pedagogical content knowledge: Teacher trainee demonstrates knowledge and capability as they create show-case age-appropriate activities and materials that engage children in manipulation, problem-solving and critical thinking [69].
sub_strands.values: Patriotism: Teacher trainee is conscious of his/her social and moral duties as they report factors influencing learning in children [70].

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to analyse cognitive theories in relation to learning
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Exhaustively analyses cognitive theories in relation to learning [70]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Analyses cognitive theories in relation to learning [70]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Analyses cognitive theories in relation to learning leaving out few details [71]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Analyses few cognitive theories in relation to learning but leaves out many details [71]

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to apply the principles of cognitive theories of learning as used to enhance learning in children
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Comprehensively applies the principles of cognitive theories of learning as used to enhance learning in children [71]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Apply the principles of cognitive theories of learning as used to enhance learning in children [71]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Applies some principles of cognitive theories of learning as used to enhance learning in children,that require clarity [72]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: All the principles of cognitive theories of learning applied to enhance learning in children, require clarity [72]

specific_learning_outcomes: examine the types of motivation and their relevance to learning [72]
apply different types of motives and motivation to stimulate learning [73]
recognise the importance of types motives and motivation in learning [73]
suggested_learning_experiences: use ICT devices and print media to research on types of motives and their implications on learning and report the findings in plenary [73]
brainstorm the types of motives (primary, stimulus and social motives), motivation (Abraham Maslow’s extrinsic and intrinsic motivation) how they relate to learning [73]
participate in a case study analysis, reviewing scenarios that showcase intrinsic and extrinsic motivators [74]
discuss the effect of motivation on learning for young children [74]
develop a motivation plan that integrates both intrinsic and extrinsic motivators tailored to different student profiles with varying needs and interests [74, 75]
organise a role-play to simulate different classroom dynamics that cater to social motives [75]
suggested_key_inquiry_questions: How can Maslow's theory of motivation be applied to cater for individual differences in learning? [74]
What are the implications of different types of motives to learning? [74]
sub_strands.core_competencies_developed: Digital Literacy: Teacher trainee access the internet for digital information as they use ICT devices to research on types of motives and their implications on learning [75].
sub_strands.values: Responsibility: Teacher trainee engages in assigned roles and duties as they organise a role-play to simulate different classroom dynamics that cater to social motives [75].

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to examine the types of motivation and their relevance to learning
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Extensively examines the types of motivation and their relevance to learning [76]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Examines the types of motivation and their relevance to learning [76]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Examine some types of motivation and partial relevance to learning [76]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Examines the types of motivation and their relevance to learning with many errors that require clarity [76]

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to apply different types of motives and motivation to stimulate learning
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Distinctly applies different types of motives and motivation to stimulate learning [77]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Applies different types of motives and motivation to stimulate learning [77]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Applies some different types of motives and motivation to stimulate learning [77]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: All types of motives and motivation to stimulate learning applied, require clarity [77]

specific_learning_outcomes: describe the principles of operant conditioning theory and its relevance in learning [78]
demonstrate operant conditioning theory in a learning environment [78]
appreciate the principles of operant conditioning theory in learning [78]
suggested_learning_experiences: search and write a report on principles of operant conditioning by B.F. Skinner and its application in learning to cater for individual differences among learners [78, 79]
organise a debate on the application of operant conditioning theory in learning [79]
watch a documentary or video clip on how the operant conditioning theory enhances learning [79]
create a scenario to act out different situations aligned to the principles of operant conditioning theory.Critique the presentations in class [79]
suggested_key_inquiry_questions: How can a teacher apply the principles of operant conditioning to encourage learning among demotivated learners? [79]
sub_strands.core_competencies_developed: Pedagogical content knowledge: Teacher trainee organises the content into effective learning as they participate in a debate on the application of operant conditioning theory during learning [79, 80].
sub_strands.values: Respect: Teacher trainee understands and appreciates others as they create a scenario to act out different situations aligned to the principles of operant conditioning theory [80].

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to describe the principles of operant conditioning theory and its relevance in learning
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Gives an in depth description of principles of operant conditioning theory and its relevance in learning [81]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Describes principles of operant conditioning theory and its relevance in learning [81]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Describes some principles of operant conditioning theory and its relevance in learning and gives a few details [81]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Describes some principles of operant conditioning theory and its relevance in learning with many distorted details [81]

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to demonstrate operant conditioning theory in a learning environment
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Comprehensively demonstrates operant conditioning theory in a learning environment [82]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Demonstrates operant conditioning theory in a learning environment [82]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Partially demonstrates operant conditioning theory in a learning environment [82]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Demonstrates operant conditioning theory in a learning environment with details that distort meaning [82]

specific_learning_outcomes: describe the principles of classical conditioning theory and its relevance in learning [83]
demonstrate classical conditioning theory in a learning environment [83]
appreciate the principles of classical conditioning theory and its relevance in learning [83]
suggested_learning_experiences: study and discuss principles of classical conditioning by Ivan Pavlov and its application in learning to cater for individual differences among learners [83, 84]
organise a debate on the pros and cons on classical conditioning theory in learning [84]
study and reflect on a case study or documentaries on the principles of classical conditioning. Write a report and present [84]
design activities and resources that can be used to embrace the principles of classical conditioning in learning [84]
suggested_key_inquiry_questions: What role does the principles of classical conditioning theory play in the learning process? [84]
sub_strands.core_competencies_developed: Citizenship and Leadership: Teacher trainee interacts successfully and respectfully with others as they design activities and resources that can be used to embrace the principles of classical conditioning in learning [85].
sub_strands.values: Integrity: Teacher trainee is committed to assigned duty as they study, reflect and write a report on a case study or documentaries on the principles of classical conditioning [85].

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to describe the principles of classical conditioning theory and its relevance in learning
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Gives an in depth description of principles of classical conditioning theory and its relevance in learning [86]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Describes principles of classical conditioning theory and its relevance in learning [86]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Partially describes some principles of classical conditioning theory and its relevance in learning [86]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Describes principles of classical conditioning theory and its relevance in learning with scanty distorted details [86]

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to demonstrate operant conditioning theory in a learning environment
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Comprehensively demonstrates operant conditioning theory in a learning environment [87]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Demonstrates operant conditioning theory in a learning environment [87]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Leaves out some details when applying different types of motives and motivation to stimulate learning [87]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Leaves out many details when applying different types of motives and motivation to stimulate learning [87]

specific_learning_outcomes: describe the importance of guidance and counselling to the learner [88]
examine socio-emotional and psychological issues [88]
examine guidance and counselling theories and their relevance to learning [88]
appreciate the guidance and counselling in assisting learners to manage emotional and psychological issues [88]
suggested_learning_experiences: use ICT devices and print media to find information on guiding and counselling and the importance of guidance and counselling to young children [89]
use documentary, case studies, or share experiences to differentiate between guidance and counselling [89]
investigate and write a report on ways of handling challenges young children face during learning [89]
brainstorm causes of socio-emotional and psychological issues (anger, grief, depression, anxiety and stress) and how they affect young learners [89]

role play a session to act out different situations aligned to counselling theories. Record and showcase the results. (person-centred theory-Carl Rogers and Cognitive behavioural therapy by Aaron Beck) [89, 90]
sub_strands.core_competencies_developed: Self-efficacy: Teacher trainee reflects confidence as they role play, record and showcase a session to act out different situations aligned to counselling theories [90].
sub_strands.values: Respect: Teacher trainee accommodates diverse opinions as they brainstorm causes of socio-emotional and psychological issues and how they affect young learners [90, 91].

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to describe the importance of guidance and counselling for the learner
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Clearly describes the importance of guidance and counselling for the learner [91]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Describes the importance of guidance and counselling for the learner [91]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Partially describes the importance of guidance and counselling for the learner [91]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Vaguely describes the importance of guidance and counselling for the learner [92]

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to examine socio-emotional and psychological issues
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Distinctively examines socio-emotional and psychological issues [92]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Examines socio-emotional and psychological issues [92]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Partly examines socio-emotional and psychological issues [92]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: All socio-emotional and psychological issues examined require clarity [92]

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to examine guidance and counselling theories and their relevance to learning
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Accurately examines guidance and counselling theories and their relevance to learning [93]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Examines guidance and counselling theories and their relevance to learning [93]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Examines few guidance and counselling theories and their relevance to learning [93]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Many guidance and counselling theories and their relevance to learning are inaccurately examined [93]

sub_strands.concept: Qualities of an Effective Teacher Counsellor
specific_learning_outcomes: describe the qualities of an effective teacher counsellor [94]
analyse counselling in relation to the learning process [94]
apply the counselling skills required during the learning process [94]
value an effective teacher counsellor in relation to the learning process [94]
suggested_learning_experiences: use ICT devices and print media to search information and research further on the qualities of an effective teacher counsellor [95]
brainstorm the basic counselling (techniques, skills, types) and write a report [95]
organise a role play depicting qualities of an effective teacher counsellor. Critique the presentations [95]
suggested_key_inquiry_questions: What key qualities make an effective teachers counsellor? [95]
sub_strands.core_competencies_developed: Learning to learn and Reflective Practice: Teacher trainee appreciates the design of an effective counselling program that reflects on management strategies for addressing emotional and psychological challenges in young children [95, 96].
sub_strands.values: Unity: Teacher trainee strives to achieve common goals as they organise and critique a role play depicting qualities of an effective teacher counsellor [96].

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to describe the qualities of an effective teacher counsellor
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Describes the qualities of an effective teacher counsellor correctly [96, 97]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Describes the qualities of an effective teacher counsellor [97]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Describes some of the qualities of an effective teacher counsellor [97]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Describes the qualities of an effective teacher counsellor with incorrect details [97]

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to analyse counselling in relation to the learning process
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Extensively analyses counselling in relation to the learning process [97]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Analyses counselling in relation to the learning process [97, 98]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Partially analyses counselling in relation to the learning process [98]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Vaguely analyses counselling in relation to the learning process, with distorted details [98]

formative_assessment_rubrics.indicator: Ability to apply the counselling skills required during the learning process
formative_assessment_rubrics.exceeds_expectation: Distinctly applies the counselling skills required during the learning process [98]
formative_assessment_rubrics.meet_expectation: Applies the counselling skills required during the learning process [98]
formative_assessment_rubrics.approaching_expectation: Leaves out some details when applying the counselling skills required during the learning process [98]
formative_assessment_rubrics.below_expectation: Vaguely applies the counselling skills required during the learning process leaving out many details [2]
